            have h₆ : DifferentiableAt ℂ (fun z : ℂ => E₄.toFun z) (Complex.I * t) := by

have h : DifferentiableAt ℝ FReal t :=
    by
      -- Since F is an analytic function, its restriction to the imaginary axis is smooth.
      -- Therefore, FReal is a smooth function of t, hence differentiable at any t > 0.
      apply DifferentiableAt.comp (𝓝 t) (differentiableAt_id) _
      -- The composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try simp_all [FReal, F]) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic to deduce that its restriction to the imaginary axis is smooth.
          apply DifferentiableAt.const_mul
          <;>
          (try
            {
              apply DifferentiableAt.const_mul
              <;>
              (try
                {
                  apply DifferentiableAt.pow
                  <;>
                  (try
                    {
                      apply DifferentiableAt.add
                      <;>
                      (try
                        {
                          apply DifferentiableAt.sub
                          <;>
                          (try
                            {
                              apply DifferentiableAt.mul
                              <;>
                              (try
                                {
                                  -- Each of E₂, E₄, E₆ is holomorphic, hence their combination is holomorphic.
                                  aesop
                                })
                            })
                        })
                    })
                })
            })
          <;>
          aesop
        })
      <;>
      aesop
  exact h

have h1 : MDifferentiableAt 𝕜 𝕜 F (↑t * Complex.I) := by
    have h2 : MDifferentiable 𝕜 𝕜 F := F_holo
    exact h2.differentiableAt
  have h3 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
    -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic and the composition with the real part is differentiable
    have h4 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.eval (↑t * Complex.I)).re) t := by
      -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic and the real part of a holomorphic function is differentiable
      have h5 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => F.eval (↑t * Complex.I)) t := by
        -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic and the composition with the real part is differentiable
        have h6 : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => F.eval (↑t * Complex.I)) (Complex.I * ((F.deriv (↑t * Complex.I)))) t := by
          -- Use the chain rule and the fact that F is holomorphic
          have h7 : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (↑t : ℂ) * Complex.I) (Complex.I) t := by
            simpa using ((hasDerivAt_id t).const_mul (1 : ℂ)).mul_const Complex.I
          have h8 : HasDerivAt F (F.deriv (↑t * Complex.I)) (↑t * Complex.I) := by
            apply DifferentiableAt.hasDerivAt
            exact h1
          have h9 : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => F.eval (↑t * Complex.I)) (Complex.I * ((F.deriv (↑t * Complex.I)))) t := by
            -- Use the chain rule
            have h10 : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (↑t : ℂ) * Complex.I) (Complex.I) t := h7
            have h11 : HasDerivAt F (F.deriv (↑t * Complex.I)) (↑t * Complex.I) := h8
            -- Use the chain rule to get the derivative
            have h12 : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => F.eval (↑t * Complex.I)) (Complex.I * ((F.deriv (↑t * Complex.I)))) t := by
              -- Apply the chain rule
              convert HasDerivAt.comp t h11 h10 using 1 <;> field_simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I] <;>
                simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I] <;>
                  ring_nf <;>
                  norm_num <;>
                  simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
            exact h12
          exact h9
        have h13 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => F.eval (↑t * Complex.I)) t := by
          apply DifferentiableAt.hasDerivAt
          exact h6
        exact h13
      -- Use the fact that the real part of a differentiable function is differentiable
      have h14 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.eval (↑t * Complex.I)).re) t := by
        apply DifferentiableAt.re
        exact h5
      exact h14
    -- The function (F.resToImagAxis t).re is the same as (F.eval (↑t * Complex.I)).re
    have h15 : (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) = (fun t : ℝ => (F.eval (↑t * Complex.I)).re) := by
      funext t
      rfl
    rw [h15]
    exact h4
  -- FReal is defined as (F.resToImagAxis t).re
  have h16 : FReal = (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) := by
    funext t
    rfl
  rw [h16]
  exact h3

    have h₃ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) t := by

          apply h₂.hasDerivAt
        have h₆ : HasDerivAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) (-(F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).im) t := by
          have h₇ : HasDerivAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) Complex.I t := by
            simpa using (hasDerivAt_id t).const_mul (Complex.I : ℂ)
          have h₈ : HasDerivAt ℂ (fun t : ℝ => F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))) (deriv F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) * Complex.I) (t : ℝ) := by
            have h₉ : HasDerivAt ℂ (fun t : ℝ => (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))) Complex.I (t : ℝ) := by
              simpa using h₇
            have h₁₀ : HasDerivAt ℂ (fun z : ℂ => F z) (deriv F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))) (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) := h₅
            have h₁₁ : HasDerivAt ℂ (fun t : ℝ => F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))) (deriv F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) * Complex.I) (t : ℝ) := by
              have h₁₂ : HasDerivAt ℂ (fun t : ℝ => Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) Complex.I (t : ℝ) := h₉
              have h₁₃ : HasDerivAt ℂ (fun z : ℂ => F z) (deriv F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))) (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) := h₁₀
              have h₁₄ : HasDerivAt ℂ (fun t : ℝ => F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))) (deriv F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) * Complex.I) (t : ℝ) :=
                h₁₃.comp (t : ℝ) h₁₂
              exact h₁₄
            exact h₁₁
          have h₁₅ : HasDerivAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) (-(F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).im) t := by
            have h₁₆ : HasDerivAt ℂ (fun t : ℝ => F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))) (deriv F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ)) * Complex.I) (t : ℝ) := h₈
            have h₁₇ : HasDerivAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) (-(F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).im) t := by
              -- Use the fact that the derivative of the real part is the negative of the imaginary part of the complex derivative
              convert HasDerivAt.re (h₁₆) using 1
              <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, mul_comm]
              <;> ring_nf
              <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, mul_comm]
              <;> norm_num
              <;> linarith
            exact h₁₇
          exact h₁₅
        exact h₆
      have h₁₈ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) t := by
        apply h₄.differentiableAt
      exact h₁₈
    have h₁₉ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) t := h₃
    have h₂₀ : (fun t : ℝ => (F (Complex.I * (t : ℂ))).re) = FReal := by
      funext t

              <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
              <;>
                (try norm_num) <;>
                (try linarith)
            })
      exact h₃
    exact h₂
  -- Now, we know that `(fun x : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis x).re)` is differentiable at `t`.
  -- But `FReal t = (F.resToImagAxis t).re`, so we can directly use this fact.
  have h₂ : FReal = (fun x : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis x).re) := by
    funext x
    rfl
  rw [h₂]
  exact h₁

have h1 : DifferentiableAt ℝ FReal t := by
    have h2 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
      have h3 : ComplexDifferentiableAt ℂ (F : ℂ → ℂ) (Complex.I * t) := by
        apply F_holo.complexDifferentiableAt
      have h4 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
        have h5 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
          have h6 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
            apply DifferentiableAt.comp (𝓝 t) (Complex.differentiableAt_re) (DifferentiableAt.of_real (h3.ofReal))
          exact h6
        exact h5
      exact h4
    have h7 : (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) = FReal := by
      funext t
      rfl
    rw [h7] at h2
    exact h2
  exact h1

have h₁ : DifferentiableAt ℝ FReal t := by
    have h₂ : Complex.differentiableAt (fun z : ℂ => F z) t := by
      have h₃ : Differentiable ℂ F := by
        exact F_holo.differentiable le_rfl
      exact h₃.differentiableAt
    have h₄ : DifferentiableAt ℝ FReal t := by
      have h₅ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun x : ℝ => (F (x * Complex.I)).re) t := by
        have h₆ : DifferentiableAt ℂ (fun x : ℝ => (x : ℂ) * Complex.I) t := by
          exact differentiableAt_id.const_mul (Complex.I)
        have h₇ : DifferentiableAt ℂ (fun z : ℂ => F z) (t * Complex.I) := by
          have h₈ : Differentiable ℂ F := by
            exact F_holo.differentiable le_rfl
          exact h₈.differentiableAt
        have h₉ : DifferentiableAt ℂ (fun x : ℝ => F (x * Complex.I)) t := by
          apply DifferentiableAt.comp (h₇) h₆
        exact h₉.differentiableAt.re
      convert h₅ using 1
      <;> simp [FReal, Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, mul_comm]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> norm_num
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, mul_comm]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> linarith
    exact h₄
  exact h₁

have h₁ : FReal t = (F.resToImagAxis t).re := by simp [FReal]
  have h₂ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
    -- Since F is holomorphic and the restriction to the imaginary axis is smooth, its real part is differentiable.
    -- Here, we use the fact that the restriction of a holomorphic function to a real line is smooth.
    have h₃ : MDifferentiable 𝓘(ℂ) 𝓘(ℂ) F := F_holo
    have h₄ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
      -- Use the fact that the real part of a holomorphic function restricted to the imaginary axis is differentiable
      -- as it is the composition of differentiable functions.
      have h₅ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
        -- This step involves detailed analysis of the function F and its restriction.
        -- For the purpose of this proof, we assume that F is sufficiently smooth.
        -- In practice, this would require a more rigorous justification.
        apply DifferentiableAt.comp (differentiableAt_id : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => t) t)
        -- Use the fact that the restriction of F to the imaginary axis is differentiable
        -- and the real part is a linear operation.
        have h₆ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
          -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof, which would involve detailed analysis of F.
          -- For now, we assume it is differentiable.
          have h₇ : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) t := by
            -- The actual proof would go here.
            -- Since F is a meromorphic function and its restriction to the imaginary axis is smooth,
            -- its real part is differentiable.
            -- However, to keep this proof simple, we use the fact that the restriction of F to the imaginary axis is differentiable.
            -- We use the holomorphy of F to deduce the differentiability of its real part.
            apply DifferentiableAt.re
            apply DifferentiableAt.comp (differentiableAt_id : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun t : ℝ => t) t)
            -- Use the fact that F is holomorphic and the restriction to the imaginary axis is differentiable.
            -- This step is non-trivial and requires a detailed analysis of F and its restriction.
            -- For simplicity, we assume it is differentiable.
            have h₈ : DifferentiableAt 𝔂 (fun z : ℂ => F z) (Complex.I * ↑t : ℂ) := by
              -- Since F is holomorphic everywhere, it is differentiable at every point.
              -- We use the fact that F is holomorphic to deduce that its restriction to the imaginary axis is differentiable.
              have h₉ : DifferentiableAt 𝔂 F (Complex.I * ↑t : ℂ) := by
                apply F_holo.differentiableAt
              exact h₉
            -- Convert the complex differentiability to real differentiability.
            -- This step uses the fact that the restriction of F to the imaginary axis is differentiable.
            -- The actual proof would involve detailed analysis of the function F and its restriction.
            have h₉ : DifferentiableAt 𝔂 (fun z : ℂ => F z) (Complex.I * ↑t : ℂ) := h₈
            convert h₉ using 1 <;>
              simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, mul_comm] <;>
                ring_nf <;>
                  norm_num <;>
                    simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I, mul_comm] <;>
                      norm_num <;>
                        linarith
          exact h₇
        exact h₆
      exact h₅
    exact h₄
  -- Since FReal t is defined as the real part of F.resToImagAxis t, and we have shown that this real part is differentiable,
  -- it follows that FReal t is differentiable at t.
  have h₃ : FReal = (fun t : ℝ => (F.resToImagAxis t).re) := by
    funext t
    simp [FReal]
  rw [h₃]
  exact h₂
